title: aquilegia vulgaris subsp. atroviolacea
intro: Aquilegia vulgaris subsp. atroviolacea is a subspecies of the common columbine plant, characterized by its dark purple flowers and distinctive shape. The plant has been used in traditional herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which make it useful for treating wounds and reducing swelling. The health benefits of this plant include its ability to support immune function, reduce stress and anxiety, and promote relaxation. Herbal preparations of Aquilegia vulgaris subsp. atroviolacea include teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
